design_philosophy.core_loop: Plan mission strategy via briefing → execute combat objectives in first-person flight → manage power allocation between weapons/engines/shields → balance multiple simultaneous tactical concerns → receive promotion and unlock advanced craft
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Asymmetric capability management: player must pilot fragile, unshielded TIE fighters early against superior Rebel forces while progressively unlocking advanced variants; tension between aggressive dogfighting and defensive resource management
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become an elite Imperial starfighter pilot rising through ranks; command respect from a tyrannical empire by outflying Rebel opponents; experience the Empire's perspective as organized, meritocratic force maintaining galactic order
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately inverts typical Star Wars morality by portraying the Empire as maintainers of peace and stability; emphasizes player competence through complex power allocation mechanics rather than hand-holding; requires mastery of multiple simultaneous systems; refuses to simplify narrative into good-vs-evil, instead anchoring player under Thrawn's tactical genius rather than Vader's terror

difficulty.lives_system: Per-mission failure system: destroyed craft before objective completion forces mission restart; destroyed craft after objectives met counts as success
difficulty.continues_system: Infinite mission attempts allowed; player retains progression and rank between attempts
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpointing; no mid-mission saves; entire mission must be completed in single flight
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through craft selection progression: early unshielded TIE variants force defensive flying and power management discipline; progression to shielded advanced fighters provides capability scaling; optional secondary/secret objectives allow skilled players to extract additional challenge and rank

progression.upgrade_system: Craft progression system: player gains access to new Imperial starfighter variants (shielded vs unshielded, different armament loadouts) as campaign advances; advanced fighters carry shields, better weapons, and specialized systems (tractor beams, jamming beams); power allocation between weapons/engines/shields becomes available only in later craft
progression.unlock_mechanism: Tour of duty structure: 13 tours divided into 4-8 sequential missions each; some tours playable out of order but missions within tours are linear; completing primary objectives advances tour; completing secondary/secret objectives from Inner Circle briefings grants additional medals and promotions in separate rank track
replayability_hooks: Combat chamber offers 4 extra historical reenactment missions per pilotable craft for training outside campaign
Training simulator provides obstacle course challenges for each Imperial craft
Mission recordings can be reviewed to improve tactics
Secondary and secret mission objectives offer additional challenge and reward branches within linear campaign
Multiple rank tracks (standard military vs Inner Circle) incentivize replaying missions for optimal objective completion

world_building_techniques: Mission briefings with animated maps showing vessel positions and flight patterns
In-flight radio communication advancing plot during combat
Dual-briefing system: primary objective briefing plus mysterious Inner Circle figure providing secondary objectives and lore exposition
Included novella (Prima Publishing strategy guide) names player character Maarek Stele and provides background narrative
Tech room accessible from concourse hub displaying information on all spacecraft appearing in game
Mission debriefings providing story consequences and advancement
Narrative framing: player serves under tactical genius Grand Admiral Thrawn rather than Darth Vader, positioning Empire as organized meritocracy

boss_design.philosophy: No traditional boss encounters documented; instead game emphasizes varied mission objectives requiring different tactical approaches: dogfighting against Rebel elite pilots, capital ship destruction requiring component targeting, escort missions, inspection/scanning objectives. Progression measured through increasingly capable enemy forces and pilot opponents rather than boss gauntlet.

faction_indicators: Imperial rank structure with two progression tracks (military promotion vs Inner Circle medals)
Service under Grand Admiral Thrawn establishing meritocratic command hierarchy
Opposition to Rebel Alliance, pirates, Imperial traitors, and civil war combatants
Emperor's Inner Circle as elite inner sanctum providing secret objectives
danger_signals: Player prevents coup against Emperor Palpatine as campaign climax, establishing loyalty stakes
Unshielded TIE fighters early in campaign represent expendability of lower-rank pilots
Enemy variety (Rebels, pirates, traitors) justifies imperial security apparatus
Service under authoritarian regime framed as maintaining peace and order
environmental_storytelling: Mission map animations showing vessel deployments and strategic positions
Spacecraft tech specifications in accessible tech room demonstrating Imperial engineering sophistication
Mission locations ranging from Hoth aftermath through Return of the Jedi timeline establishing galactic scope
Craft progression (unshielded → shielded, basic → advanced weaponry) visually represents pilot advancement through ranks
Concourse hub design establishing military base as player home base
